question: How is PII/sensitive data governed?
resolution: Governance-by-disclosure: Discovery classifies data_sensitivity (tier0/tier1/tier2); Tier-2 = fixtures-only during build, real data connected post-handoff in the NGO's own environment; triage enforces.

question: What is the deliverable and who builds it?
resolution: A DEPLOYED app the non-technical NGO maintains via chat. Track A: Lovable is the durable home; Claude Code orchestrates via LovableDriver port; ai4good Skill is v1 CORE.
rejected: Repo-as-deliverable; Lovable as a side track

question: Can fuel be cashed out?
resolution: No cash-out in v1. Fuel is prepaid, non-cash-refundable, project-scoped credit; leftover becomes redeployable credit (keep or donate); move-between-projects is v1.5.

question: What legal entity?
resolution: Nonprofit path: fiscal sponsorship now, own 501(c)(3) later.

question: Unit economics for v1?
resolution: Blended sustainability: skim + grants + donations; v1 accepted as net-negative on skim alone; bottom-up year-1 P&L ordered.

question: Launch strategy?
resolution: Concierge-first: pre-recruited 20-30 volunteer bench, hand-match first 10-15 curated projects before organic browse. Supply liquidity is the #1 gate.

question: Legal framing of the no-guarantee posture?
resolution: Real counsel-reviewed ToS carrying no-warranty/no-SLA/limitation-of-liability; warm coordination-layer voice in UI.

question: Who consumes fuel and when can it be funded?
resolution: Unified fuel: NGO (Discovery, discovery_consumption kind) and volunteer (consumption) both draw project fuel at 15% skim; funding allowed from draft onward; email verification is the Discovery bot floor; NGO-doc verification is the publish wall.

question: Where does the PM task tree live?
resolution: SUPERSEDED BY d20. (Was: git-as-truth tasks.json via local TaskMaster + webhook projection.)

question: Task-tree simplification pass?
resolution: Six consolidations, ~-80h: one meter seam, one notify() comms substrate + EVENT_MAP, one outbox + idempotent handler registry with flat retry, defer concierge CRM + public-beta hardening slice.
rejected: Bespoke DLQ state machine; tuned backoff ladders

question: Credit model for the free Discovery layer?
resolution: Two-layer money: dollar-pegged fuel + abstract context-weighted Discovery credits; DAILY drip 10/day unverified, 30/day verified, hard reset 00:00 UTC, no rollover; 'Funded -> all-$' routing (positive-fuel projects bill Discovery to fuel); credits NGO-bound, fuel project-bound; honesty rule: funding removes the wall, never 'faster'.

question: Post-Discovery NGO chat?
resolution: REQ-033: funded projects unlock a read-only fuel-metered project assistant on the same chat surface (mode marker); never mutates; scope asks route to draft CRs.
rejected: New table/ledger kind; proactive digests in v1

question: Model for Discovery + assistant?
resolution: Opus (not Sonnet): highest-leverage reasoning step; credits are token-based/model-agnostic so allowance stays 10/30.

question: Spend-anomaly engine in v1?
resolution: Deferred to v1.5. Loss ceiling holds via deterministic caps + no cash-out + first-fund cap + fuel gate + daily human review of the money dashboard.

question: Project communication surface?
resolution: Lightweight project comment thread on task_comments (task_id NULL = project-level); system events -> notifications via notify(); full Slack-style channel deferred to v1.5.
rejected: Realtime channel tables/markdown/@-mentions in v1; full cut of NGO-volunteer free-text

question: CR distraction protection?
resolution: CR stays in_progress-only + guardrails: low-tone cr_raised (no email), one open CR per project (unique partial index), penalty-free Decline, NGO framing at submission. Only Accept creates work.
rejected: Broadening CR availability; real-time CR interrupts

question: Paused state in v1?
resolution: Removed: 10 lifecycle states; unpublish (open->scoped) is the only take-off-marketplace path; mid-build stops are support actions or cancel; pause/resume v1.5 on first real request.
rejected: paused state + clock-freeze stash columns + cron special-casing

question: Platform stack?
resolution: TanStack Start (SSR) on Lovable hosting + Supabase Edge Functions (Deno) + pg_cron; single repo, Lovable owns src/, Claude Code owns supabase/ and planning dirs; external Supabase, not Lovable Cloud.

question: Track B in v1?
resolution: Deferred post-v1: v1 is Track-A-only; non-A needs waitlisted at Discovery; Lovable mandatory (skip opt-out removed); 100% Lovable dependency accepted; enums reserved for no-migration reinstatement.
rejected: Building Track B with a weaker promise; rejecting non-A needs outright

question: Task system of record?
resolution: Linear (reverses d9) on real-signals grounds: free workspace per project; coordinator-owned decomposition (automation drafts, human approves, API push); pull-based self-assignment; status ONLY via GitHub integration on PR merge; detect-and-revert; linear-webhook -> one-writer Postgres projection; NGO sees panel only; CI tree snapshot at handoff; coordination infra platform-owned, never transfers.
rejected: git-as-truth tasks.json (agent-authored status violates real-signals); wrapper-MCP as primary (held in reserve)

question: Linear workspace creation (no public API) on the kickoff critical path?
resolution: Pool amendment: concierge batch pre-creates ready workspaces (API key + webhook + GitHub integration pre-connected); kickoff assigns via organizationUpdate rename, API-only; watermark linear_pool_replenish ops_task (default 3); pool-empty is the only stall mode. Fair-use note: bulk empty workspaces sharpen the need for Linear's written blessing.
rejected: Per-kickoff manual workspace creation (~5 min on the critical path)

question: Token governance for volunteer compute?
resolution: LLM gateway ships in v1 (pulls AI-Proxy-v1.5 forward): a4g_* virtual keys per volunteer x project (hash-only, show-once), caps (per-request + rolling 24h), fingerprint tripwire >~1.5k tokens, governance-prompt injection, inline per-request ledger writes (gateway_request_id idempotency), structural fuel gate, instant one-row revocation; real key only in gateway env; bodies never persisted; escalation ladder documented-not-built. Hosting = OD-6.
rejected: Per-project Anthropic workspaces + manual Console keys + usage poller + Cost-Report reconciliation + deactivation saga + multi-org router

question: Attribution and transparency scope for v1?
resolution: Capture in v1: REQ-034 task-ref binding on every ledger row (PostToolUse hook -> .a4g/task.md -> gateway extraction; floor unattributed, never gates; exploration/onboarding buckets; NGO burn-per-deliverable) + REQ-035 handoff attribution (testimonial + 3 credit-framed dimensions, supersedes no-satisfaction-form) + 30/60/90 jumpstart health with 60-day tried-and-failed confound question. Synthesis/matching surfaces v1.5.
rejected: Deferring capture (uncaptured data is lost forever); public star ratings (permanently out)
source: governance design thread + founder scope call

title: Single delivery model - Track B removed altogether
decision: Supersedes d19 deferral: track taxonomy deleted. One model: NGO-self-maintainable tool, Lovable durable home, Claude-Code-orchestrated build. Developer-grade waitlist deleted; needs failing the maintainability fit check are declined at Discovery with plain messaging, declines recorded for founder review (visibility only).
source: founder review comment on prd-new artifact

title: Review-batch 2 corrections
decision: (a) kickoff decomposition gated by coordinator review (one human gate, no admin ops tasks); (b) Skill ENFORCES pull-then-complete (explicit task-or-exploration binding before substantial work; completion = explicit dev act driving the merge); (c) handoff repo-transfer step removed (repo stays in platform org permanently); (d) Discovery file attachment/reads cost zero credits, no confirm interruption (daily allowance is the spend ceiling).
source: founder line-comments batch 2 on prd-new artifact

title: Dev-authored project PRD + automated completion gate
decision: Discovery output = scope contract, never decomposed directly. Kickoff seeds one bootstrap task: volunteer authors the project PRD from Discovery (clarifications via REQ-024 blockers). Automated scorer measures PRD completion vs Discovery; only >= threshold (OD-7, pilot-tuned) lets the project progress; build tree decomposed from the gated PRD (new REQ-036). Coordinator per-kickoff review retires to pilot spot-check (supersedes decomposition half of d24a). Scoring runs fuel-metered. Mirrors the ai4good buildout methodology itself.

title: MVP simplification pass (codex-debated)
decision: 12 cuts debated adversarially with codex (max 3 rounds each; all core cuts conceded, minimal guards forced): cadence minimal, badges->tags, cash-buffer alert->policy, canary/self-audit->v1.5 + org-settings guard, notif batching->v1.5 + atomic-outbox guard, blocker uniform rule + per-instance owner + Tier-2 warnings, observability=one money dashboard + all-surfaces metering audit invariant, runbooks 6->3 + hardened breach card, file-sync command->v1.5, money corrections = one privileged idempotent validating function (no UI). Founder verdicts folded: collusion detection REMOVED, admin credit grants REMOVED, AUP saga -> plain deactivate+revoke. PENDING founder confirm: c7 REQ-033 assistant deferral (reverses d12), c8 REQ-035 60/90 trim (trims d22).
source: founder MVP directive + codex adversarial debate wf_19ff9ac3-343

title: c7/c8 founder resolutions
decision: c7 REJECTED by founder: REQ-033 NGO assistant STAYS in v1 (decision-12 stands). c8 CONFIRMED: decision-22 amended - 60/90-day health layer + email to v1.5; v1 keeps 30-day reachability ping + attribution + structured day-45-60 founder check-in (required ops item at handoff acceptance, six-field PII-minimizing closed form, 2-business-day follow-up SLA).

title: Public-only v1; private repos removed
decision: Entire private-project feature dropped from v1 - all projects public MIT, no visibility choice, no Private badge, no private-repo justification/triage, no restricted project-page view. Confidential-codebase needs declined at Discovery (future feature). Orthogonal to data sensitivity: Tier-2 sensitive DATA still served via fixtures-only (public code, real data post-handoff). Retained as admin mechanics (not user features): org-namespace guard (member repos born private, flipped public after setup validation) + founder break-glass repo-hide (emergency takedown).
source: founder directive - drop the entire private thing, advanced feature for future

title: Second MVP-cut round (codex-evaluated, founder-ruled)
decision: s1 APPROVED-as-modified: organic marketplace (browse/filter/apply/applicant queue) to v1.5; v1 = concierge with ADMIN ENFORCE-MATCH (binding, no NGO approve/decline; volunteer one-click consent fires first-match disclaimer BEFORE any access; NGO per-match ack rides the funding screen); public read-only listings; admin match log feeds Goal 5. s2 APPROVED + founder extension: single-seat NGO accounts (invites/roles v1.5; authority attestation, no shared credentials, audited contact-transfer, escalation contact) AND single-dev projects (one volunteer per project, teams post-v1). s4 APPROVED-total-removal: donation flow gone from PRD entirely; leftover credit auto-applies at any funding checkout (can satisfy $50 min); release = ledger op under control totals. s3 REJECTED (EU/UK VAT + Stripe Tax stay v1). s5 REJECTED (Google OAuth stays).
source: founder rulings on codex-evaluated suggestions wf_578fddfe-1bf

title: Third MVP round + flow verdicts
decision: Flow #7 audited-reversal req REMOVED; #8 handoff-dispute review REMOVED. r1 APPROVED: structured CRs -> v1.5 (amends d16; informal comment-thread protocol, task-before-spend, top-priority-or-acknowledged, one-discussion rule, sensitivity re-triage). r2 APPROVED: public profile+badge -> v1.5 (append-only credit events, private handoff confirmation, honest copy). r3 APPROVED: verification machinery -> ONE audited founder-vetted action (no public verified claim, public-evidence-preferred PII rule, v1.5 gate before non-concierge admission). r4 FOUNDER REDESIGN: automated triage screener - confident-clean auto-approves + auto audit record; Tier-2 never auto; non-decided -> founder exception queue (return-to-scoped vs terminal-decline); daily-review spot-check; OD-8 threshold. r5 REJECTED: in-app notification center STAYS in v1.
source: founder one-by-one rulings, codex round wf_f9edfc79-fab

title: Provider truth drives gauges, tight reconciliation + flow 9 removal
decision: Fuel gauge = Anthropic-reported usage per gateway response (real-time by construction); aggregate reconciliation vs Anthropic usage/cost reporting on tight pull (minutes, pilot-tuned) instead of nightly; internal control totals stay nightly. Lovable meter: v1 manual widget + consent session reads (no API - Q7); STANDING TRIGGER to switch to pulled provider data when Lovable exposes usage programmatically. Flow #9 goodwill refund REMOVED ENTIRELY - zero refunds in v1, wronged NGOs made whole in spendable credit, only money-out = Stripe dispute rail.

title: Fully automatic ledger corrections (flow #10 removed)
decision: No human correction step. Tight-cadence reconciliation auto-conforms ledger to provider truth: deterministic hierarchy (Stripe wins money-in; Anthropic wins AI spend; pairing arithmetic resolves internal gaps); corrections auto-post via the guarded idempotent function (audit rows, dashboard visibility, large drift pages AFTER applying). Backstop: undecidable drift (provider data missing/contradictory) never guessed - books untouched, pages for visibility. Supersedes d26/c12 admin-invoked procedure.

title: Manual-flows session closed - all 25 resolved
decision: APPROVED backstops: #3 chargeback adjudication, #12 discovery-failure escalation, #13 DLQ, #14 incident command (steps scripted one-click), #15 blocker 7d rung. REMOVED: #2,#4,#5,#7,#8,#9,#10 (+#1 resolved by d25 redesign). MOOT: #6 (d27 public-only). POSTPONED later-stage: #11 reserve, #16 DMCA docs (CSAM statutory duty remains by law), #17 counsel (gates public launch; pilot on draft ToS), #18 Anthropic negotiation, #19 P&L cadence, #20 Linear blessing (gates pool scale-out), #23 extra runbooks. FIRE-AT-MOMENT: #21 ODs, #22 launch setup, #24 pool VERIFY, #25 pilot runs.

title: d32 scope correction
decision: Founder postponed ONLY #16 (DMCA/CSAM docs), not the #17-#25 batch. Counsel-timing and Linear-blessing edits REVERTED in prd-new; #17-#25 remain open for one-by-one rulings.

title: Flow session paused at #21
decision: #17 counsel, #18 Anthropic negotiation, #19 P&L cadence, #20 Linear blessing - ALL POSTPONED post-MVP. #21 OD register through #25 pilot runs remain open; session paused by founder for a full MVP-PRD review pass.

title: Handoff reframed as access-transition, not delivery
decision: Under enforced git-bound Lovable (d23), the live app + repo are the NGO's throughout the build - so handoff delivers/transfers NOTHING. Handoff = (1) access change: NGO removes the dev or drops to read-only (write ends, keys terminate, Linear membership removed, NGO removes from Lovable workspace) + (2) bookkeeping (attribution, completion credit, fuel release, ->handed_off). Quality checklist is a completion confirmation, not a delivery gate (app already live). Story 6 + REQ-012 reframed.

title: Discovery scope guardrails (no drip of unrelated execution)
decision: Discovery + REQ-033 assistant scope-guard conversations on free credits AND funded fuel: (1) platform-side system-prompt scope line - decline unrelated tasks (general Q&A, drafting, translation, coding help), redirect to scoping - mirrors the gateway governance line; (2) deterministic per-conversation turn ceiling (platform-configurable, pilot-tuned) - past it, wrap-up + generate-scope-or-start-fresh; (3) repeated off-topic declines -> plain NGO notice + founder-visibility flag in daily review. Visibility, never lockout.

title: d34 scope corrected: FREE PHASE ONLY
decision: Founder ruling: scope guardrails (prompt scope line, turn ceiling, off-topic visibility flag) apply ONLY while Discovery runs on free daily credits - they protect the platform subsidy. Funded Discovery and the REQ-033 assistant carry NO scope guardrail: paid usage is the NGO's own call; the per-turn cost display + fuel gauge are the controls.

title: Platform Lovable member seat; offboarding automated
decision: Setup checklist adds one invite: platform ops account joins each Lovable workspace as build-phase member. Seat powers automated volunteer removal at handoff/abandonment/AUP deactivation; at handoff the platform removes itself (last one out - zero post-handoff access, when real NGO data lives in Lovable; build-phase exposure bounded by Tier-2 fixtures-only). Narrow exception to no-Lovable-access posture: member management only, no usage/billing reads. VERIFY on first pilot: member add/remove/self-remove via Lovable API/MCP; fallback = manual NGO removal with notice. Risk-table mitigation updated.

title: Volunteer candidacy precedes matching; GitHub link mandatory at signup
decision: Amends d28: volunteers mark interest in-product (candidate for this project) on public listings; each open project gets an assimilation window (platform-configurable, pilot-tuned) to gather candidates before the concierge enforce-match draws from the accumulated pool. Candidacies feed the admin match log only - no NGO-facing queue, NGO never sees candidacies. REQ-011 listings gain exactly one volunteer action (mark interest); otherwise still read-only. GitHub link becomes mandatory at volunteer signup (supersedes d28 at-first-consent timing; no unlinked volunteer accounts); platform GitHub-org membership still waits for first match consent so repo-creation rights are never granted to never-matched volunteers.
source: founder review comment on REQ-007 (halved prd-mvp artifact)

title: Assimilation window = time-based, clock starts at first candidacy
decision: Founder ruling: the d36 assimilation window is TIME-based, not count-based, and its clock starts at the FIRST candidacy. A candidate-less project just stays open (Goal-5 unmatched-aging still applies); the first candidate starts the countdown; later candidates join the pool without resetting the clock; at lapse the concierge matches from the accumulated pool. Duration platform-configurable, pilot-tuned.

title: Assimilation window backs off per candidate - interest expedites matching
decision: Founder ruling: each additional candidacy SHRINKS the remaining assimilation window (backoff factor pilot-tuned). Accumulating interest expedites the match and never extends the wait; strong interest converges toward near-immediate concierge matching. The clock still starts at the first candidacy; candidate-less projects stay open under Goal-5 aging.

title: NGO-only bot interface on the project page
decision: The project page stays publicly readable and role-uniform in its content (task tree, cadence, activity), but the interactive assistant/bot (REQ-033) is surfaced ONLY to the project's NGO account - never to the assigned volunteer, platform visitors, or the public. Rationale: the assistant spends the NGO's fuel and answers the NGO's status questions; volunteers and the public get the page content, not a bot. Amends REQ-010 (adds the one viewer-specific element) and REQ-033 (NGO-only surfacing).
source: founder review comment on REQ-010 (pure prd-mvp artifact)

title: GitHub-style project activity UI; cadence = task progression + commits
decision: Fold in a GitHub-repo-view-style activity UI for the project page + in-progress showcase card (languages/stack bar, updated-N-ago, activity sparkline, contributor, license, handoff-readiness %, cause tags). CADENCE REDEFINED: liveness is measured from BOTH PM task progression (Linear task-completion events) AND commit activity, not commits alone. Open-project listing card stays static attributes only (no repo yet). Popularity metrics (stars/forks/watchers) are NEVER shown - reputation is completion credit, not popularity (Promise 5, Out-of-Scope 11), and interest/candidacy is admin-only (d36). Amends REQ-010 (cadence + repo-derived activity signals) + REQ-011 (open card fields + in-progress showcase). UI-mimic design detail in architecture-notes Cross-cutting C.
source: founder review comment on REQ-011 + research request (mimic GitHub UI)

title: Workspace-backed money model: metering out of the gateway, into Anthropic per-project workspaces
decision: Adopt the hybrid workspace-backed money path (partially reverses d21). Each project is isolated in its own Anthropic Workspace (created via the Admin API at kickoff, archived at handoff/cancellation, retained through abandonment/rematch so the successor inherits it). The per-project MONEY-OF-RECORD becomes Anthropic's billed cost per workspace (Usage/Cost API group_by=workspace_id) - NOT reconstructed from local token math; the 15% margin is recognized on that billed cost. The prepaid fuel maps to the workspace's provider spend limit (raised on top-up), so the hard fuel ceiling is provider-enforced. The GATEWAY IS RETAINED for its other roles - key confinement (volunteer never sees the workspace key), governance-prompt injection, project-binding tripwire, and the real-time zero-fuel gate trigger - but its inline token estimate is demoted to a live preview + gate trigger, never the ledger. This resolves the fragility of token-count metering (founder: token calc against the meter is fragile). 100-workspaces/org cap is not a blocker: archive-on-handoff (archived don't count) makes it a concurrent-in-flight limit; create new Anthropic orgs if approached; negotiate a cap lift once the platform gains traction. Touches REQ-009 (money path + workspace isolation + fuel gate), REQ-006 (ledger anchor now per-project), REQ-005.5 (kickoff provisions workspace), REQ-012 (handoff archives workspace), NFR workspace-cap note un-retired, Out-of-Scope #20 (provisioning now in v1) + #22 (Anthropic-side budget enforcement now used). architecture-notes Cross-cutting A (new workspace layer) + B (anchor per-project).

title: Provider-native caps suffice: retire custom gateway usage caps + OD-3
decision: Founder: the provider cap and rate limit suffice at this stage. Drop the gateway's custom per-key usage caps (per-request max + rolling-24h window sized 3-4x a heavy human day + 80/100% soft/hard + coordinator override). Budget ceiling = the Anthropic workspace SPEND LIMIT (= prepaid fuel, provider-enforced, d39); velocity/runaway = the workspace RATE LIMITS (RPM/TPM, provider-enforced). v1 adds no custom gateway caps; a light gateway anomaly-brake can return later if needed. OD-3 (volunteer spend-cap + low-fuel warning levels) RETIRED - spend-cap resolved to provider-native; the 20/5/0% warning levels are already fixed. Threat-posture line updated (spend limit bounds exposure, rate limits bound velocity). Touches REQ-009 caps bullet + threat posture, s9 OD register (OD-3 removed), architecture-notes Cross-cutting A layer 3 + tuning list.

title: Fuel state + thresholds read from Anthropic Admin API (single source of truth); provider enforces the zero-fuel stop, gateway proxies
decision: Founder: the 20/5/0% thresholds + notifications are a platform tool that reads the single source of truth = the Anthropic Admin/usage API (per-workspace spend vs limit). The gateway does NOT fail requests at 0% - Anthropic's workspace spend limit declines them and the gateway just proxies the rejection to the user. Removes the gateway's role as fuel-gate enforcer (reverses the d39 wording 'gateway inline as immediate path / gate trigger'). Gateway per-request usage capture survives ONLY as attribution telemetry (REQ-034), never the money ledger and never the gate. Fixes the Story-4 'never polling-based' contradiction (the monitor DOES poll the Admin API on a tight cadence). Touches REQ-009 (opening pipeline, money path, fuel thresholds, 401 semantics), REQ-006 (ledger = provider-truth single source + provider-enforced ceiling), Story 4 (s2), architecture-notes Cross-cutting A layer 5 + B.
source: founder review comment on REQ-009 fuel thresholds

title: Enforcement-placement wording aligned with d41 (two platform-controlled surfaces)
decision: Founder question surfaced imprecision: REQ-009 said durable enforcement lives in the gateway, but post-d41 the fuel/spend/rate enforcement moved to the Anthropic workspace. Clarified: the principle (policy cannot live in volunteer-editable files or third-party RBAC) holds, but there are now TWO platform-controlled surfaces - the GATEWAY (key confinement, binding tripwire, governance-prompt injection) and the PROVIDER-WORKSPACE LIMITS the platform sets via the Admin API (spend + rate, enforced provider-side but platform-configured, volunteer cannot override). Touches REQ-009 enforcement-placement paragraph + architecture-notes placement doctrine.
source: founder question on REQ-009 enforcement placement

title: Pull Lovable credit status via the Lovable MCP (get_workspace); RM-58 standing trigger met
decision: Research: the Lovable MCP get_workspace returns plan + available credits, and the Lovable MCP is now a standard offering across all plan tiers (no longer labeled Research Preview), OAuth-authenticated. So the RM-58/decision-30 standing trigger (switch from manual to pulled when Lovable exposes programmatic usage) is MET. FOLD: Lovable credit status is read programmatically from Lovable via the volunteer's connected Lovable session (client-side, consent-gated - Q7 path, no new access, no decision-35 change), with a manual report as FALLBACK (Lovable MCP has no documented SLA/rate limit). ai4good computes + surfaces low/exhausted in-platform and notifies the NGO. Top-up stays a Lovable billing action (deep-link; may suggest Lovable auto-top-up) - no programmatic purchase, no Lovable-native credit webhook. DEFERRED: a background/standing poll for between-session alerts would need the platform ops seat to hold its own Lovable OAuth + read billing, widening decision-35's member-management-only scope. Touches REQ-021 (status pulled + AC), REQ-010 (standing trigger now met), REQ-024 (blocker type), Q7 (s9); architecture-notes REQ-021.
source: founder research request on Lovable credit comms + correction that the MCP is no longer Research Preview

title: Lovable MCP automation map: automate kickoff provisioning, governance-into-Lovable, handoff live-URL capture; member-mgmt not available (manual offboarding)
decision: Studied the full Lovable MCP surface and mapped it to current workflows. FOLDED automation wins (all run in the volunteer connected session, no new access): (a) kickoff auto-provisions the Lovable project (create_project) + its database (enable_database) instead of a manual checklist; (b) governance-into-Lovable - ai4good injects its conventions + reviewer skill into the Lovable project via set_project_knowledge/create_workspace_skill so Lovable own agent follows them (closes the ungoverned-Lovable-agent gap, soft-norm); (c) handoff live deployment URL captured automatically via deploy_project (manual paste fallback), + get_project screenshot for showcase/review; (d) build review via get_diff/list_files/read_file; DB-state verifiable via get_database_status/query_database. CANNOT do (stay manual/UI): add_connector is UI-only (GitHub/Stripe connectors stay dashboard steps); NO member-management tools -> automated offboarding NOT possible (resolves the old [VERIFY]) -> NGO manually removes the volunteer AND the platform ops seat at handoff (the seat also cannot self-remove); workspace creation + credit top-up stay NGO-side. TWO OPEN DECISIONS flagged (not folded): (1) post-handoff 30-day health via Lovable analytics (get_project_analytics/trend) -> real adoption signal but needs a persistent platform Lovable connection (widens Q7/seat scope); (2) platform ops seat fate (revisits d35) - its offboarding purpose is unachievable via MCP; drop it or keep it only to hold the platform OAuth for #1. Touches REQ-021 (setup auto-provision + governance + offboarding), REQ-012 (URL auto-capture), REQ-021 ACs; architecture-notes Cross-cutting D.
source: founder request to study the Lovable MCP doc for automation opportunities
